tweets.text:
We are so unbelievably early with agents right now. The majority of companies aren’t even using coding agents at scale, let alone for the rest of knowledge work. We’re still mostly in the chatbot era of work for most of AI right now. 

Diffusion of tech takes time, even in the
codex hackathons have such great builder energy
time to follow: https://t.co/wPHHa7hIsw https://t.co/D1W3XgB5Am

This is another outstanding theoretical step towards understanding intelligence by Pedro, aka @AdaptiveAgents 

Some people still like to try to explain it all through RL, but I feel other explanations that emphasise the role of the environment, entropy minimisation, (multi)

Protected Spaces with Public URLs now available on Hugging Face

You can now set your Spaces to protected, making them private on Hugging Face while still keeping their URL publicly accessible

This is useful for deploying production-ready demos or internal tools without exposing https://t.co/xKlIKOlxnV

Super excited to share I'll be a keynote speaker at AI Engineer Europe in a couple of weeks in London

Who will be there?

https://t.co/NtTP3B0ySZ
“If your $500K engineer isn’t burning at least $250k in tokens, something is wrong.” https://t.co/txe0KlU8wJ

is talking to a model fine-tuned on your own thoughts (say, for investment judgement) a sign of madness? given this kind of iteration can help models, maybe it helps humans

"silicon ducking" instead of rubber ducking
The #NVIDIAGTC robotics rap recap you didn’t know you needed. 🎤 🤖 https://t.co/J7d19UoHOM

This is crazy... he used Claude to turn his daughter's piano practice into Guitar Hero

Education will never be the same 🤯
https://t.co/99eRGzBPZB

It was an honor to hang out with Jensen Huang, CEO of @nvidia, and do a long-form podcast with him. Really fun & fascinating technical deep-dive conversation on & off the mic. One of the most brilliant & thoughtful human beings I've ever met. NVIDIA is the most valuable company https://t.co/s2rOUYmPMg

First place this will land is the UI layer

Non-tech users will vibecode custom UIs that sit on existing data models for bespoke workflows.

Cost/time to dynamically assemble workflow UIs will plummet another 100x in a year; bottleneck will soon be on the product side.
I feel my main velocity limitation lately isn't token speed anymore, it's compute. Running tests in parallel is taxing; can't wait for better cloud worker integration.
rumors are frontier training runs are up from 2-4 months to 3-7. if it ends up taking us exactly 9 months to birth agi im going to mass bc something is clearly fucking with us

They looked into the "they silently changed the model!!" And how to detect it. Interesting study.

Though they only do it for API endpoints, and the usual complaint is for the chat interface; would be interesting (but harder) to extend to that.
Indeed, we have now entered the EGI "Era of General Improvement." We will be able to improve or remake pretty much anything, making them better, more efficient, and cheaper!
I will comment on this and other topics at the Infornation Bottleneck podcast. https://t.co/OxTtXCVUuZ

Anthropic showed older (2022) LLMs will give you less accurate answers if you seem less educated to the AI, but this issue has, as far as I know, been addressed in more recent models. https://t.co/iXnlBpYXLA https://t.co/ByclHjwywR

With AI coding, it’s possible every app / website becomes an App Store.

The second and third order effects of this are interesting to think about.
AI will disrupt and reinvent every industry. Its impact will be defined by continuous 3I: innovation, iteration, and improvement.
Really made tests a focus the last few days and now OpenClaw's harness takes around 2 minutes, earlier this week it was closer to 10 mins.
Codex team is busy with pretty much an end to end rethink of how everything works to scale with future model advances and I’m glad we have Codex to help refactor it all or it would take months.

AI tools for academic work have become very exciting. There are now so many outstanding AI-related repos on GitHub now, its like treasure trove for productivity & vibe coding. Past few months, my own private repos also multiplied exponentially, committing & forking almost daily!
watching claude try to use the browser...are websites being adversarial to computer use on purpose? or is CUA still that bad

Lobsters have arrived to help with research! EurekaClaw is built by @QuanquanGu UCLA AGI lab and could have a big impact! It scrapes arXiv, generates lemmas & theorems, proves ideas, writes LaTeX papers, runs experiments, all from your chat or terminal. Open source. Local-first.
any nontechnical folks want to get more comfortable/powerful in their use of AI and want to be a beta user on something I made?

Say you’re Alphabet and you’ve got $73 Billion/year in free cashflow coming in.

I think you can make the argument you’d be crazy to _not_ spend it on AI infrastructure.

It’s either that, stock buybacks, or increasing your cash reserves.

Groups of agents don’t magically sort out the unreliability of individual agents. 

Instead, they often get stuck.

Very different philosophies for skills in Codex versus Claude Code

OpenAI seems to conceive of skills functionally, mostly matter-of-fact technical references for Codex. Claude skills are more about giving the AI approaches to problems

See the difference in skill creator skills https://t.co/Gq3hcf1In0

We recently worked with The Yomiuri Shimbun to analyze more than a million social media posts to map out state-sponsored information campaigns.

https://t.co/rlYs43ywrE

Keyword searches are fragile for modern OSINT. To fix this, our team used an ensemble of different LLMs
It seems inevitable that LLMs will soon surpass human physicists at creating new theories. The next big breakthrough in theoretical physics will likely come from someone prompting a model.
